title: ferray-ufunc: rayon parallelism is defined but never used in ops
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

parallel.rs defines a thread pool, thresholds (THRESHOLD_MEMORY_BOUND = 100k, THRESHOLD_COMPUTE_BOUND = 50k), and a maybe_parallel() dispatcher. But none of the actual ufunc operations in ops/ call maybe_parallel or use rayon. All operations run single-threaded via iter().map().collect(). The parallel infrastructure exists but is dead code. NumPy doesn't parallelize ufuncs itself (defers to BLAS/OpenMP), but ferray claims rayon parallelism as a feature. Either wire the parallel dispatch into the hot paths (at least arithmetic and transcendentals for large arrays) or remove the dead code and the misleading feature claim.
